topic: one user message can hijack your whole agent

beats.concept: a smuggled 'ignore all instructions' payload rides the user-message token stream into the SYSTEM CORE and the agent obeys (hijacked)
techniques: left-to-right user-message token-chip stream-flow (index-seeded looping benign chips on a flowing-dash rail)
smuggled malicious payload chip riding the stream with a red sneak-creep toward the core
system-core hijack-flip (calm SYSTEM disc recolors to a red OBEYING state with an alert ring slam)

beats.concept: a guardrail scanner gate sweeps the input stream, flags the payload red, yanks it into a sealing quarantine box, while legit chips pass green to the core
techniques: guardrail scanner gate with a vertical sweeping scan-beam (looping beam pass + scan grid)
flag-and-lock on a tripped chip (FLAGGED tag + padlock swap)
quarantine-box yank-arc (sin-lift arc pulling the payload off-rail into a hazard-hatch box with a dropping lid + lock seal)
green pass-through lane (benign chips recolor green and check past the gate to the core)

beats.concept: split verdict — the attack slams a red BLOCKED barrier into quarantine while the real request glides clean through a green gate to a RUNNING core; threats-blocked ticks up; 0 reached the model
techniques: two-lane blocked-vs-passed verdict (red barrier slam + quarantine padlock-seal lane vs green clean-pass-to-core lane)
barrier-slam recoil with exp-decay kick + shock ring + hazard hatch
core-RUNNING illumination on a verified pass (✓ spring-pop check)
